mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter expansion rate) signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings capacity. Positive GDP growth surprises relative to trend produce positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as equity valuations expand on improved growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 vintage 23770.976 reflects ongoing expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters produce negative average 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity risk appetite. Sustained FEDFUNDS levels above 4.0% produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as discount rates rise and growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.09% indicates restrictive stance
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns exceeds -0.05 (less negative than predicted).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate increases above 4.5% signal labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. Rising unemployment triggers equity repricing downward as forward earnings expectations decline due to lower aggregate demand and consumer confidence compression over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or correlation between UNRATE level and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth deceleration combined with housing starts (HOUST) decline signals weakening consumer demand and housing investment. Joint contraction in these demand proxies predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward across consumer-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2% AND HOUST < 1300 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 732010M, HOUST at 1307K shows demand softness
failure_condition: When both RSAFS growth < 0.2% and HOUST < 1300K, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC interaction < max(IC_RSAFS, IC_HOUST)).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. Declining INDPRO over a 3-month window predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity markets reprice on deteriorating production data and forward profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised to 101.6247 from vintage 103.9203 indicates downward revision risk
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% over 3 months, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 3.5% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals persistent inflation and restrictive monetary policy. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real discount rates remain elevated and stagflation risk increases.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 324.368 with FEDFUNDS at 4.09% creates restrictive environment
regime_hint: stagflation or high-inflation monetary tightening regime
failure_condition: When both inflation > 3.5% YoY and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction signal shows lower predictive power than FEDFUNDS alone (Rank IC interaction < IC_FEDFUNDS).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth deceleration below 100K monthly additions signals labor market cooling and reduced consumer income growth. Sustained weak payroll growth over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline due to lower consumer spending and corporate hiring pullback.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised to 158472K from vintage 159540K shows downward revision
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or late-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: When 3-month average PAYEMS growth < 100K, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 54% of instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS growth rate and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration combined with retail sales (RSAFS) strength signals robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence. Joint expansion in these demand proxies produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates and equity risk premiums compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show expansion with GDPC1 at 23770.976 and RSAFS at 732010M
regime_hint: strong demand expansion or mid-cycle acceleration
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 growth > 2.0% and RSAFS growth > 0.3%, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows lower predictive power than GDPC1 alone (Rank IC interaction < IC_GDPC1).
